content: We can frame Self-Refine, the no-training LLM output refinement framework, as a single-perspective primitive self-reflection mechanism that can be upgraded by integrating multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection research to create a stronger zero-resource refinement system. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no supervised data, extra training, or external test cases while addressing its limitation of being constrained by the model's own blind spots from single-perspective feedback.
